raw_output:
33,787
- **Medium — mutable, unsigned external skill pin.** [CLAUDE.md:53](/workspaces/sleep-optimizer/CLAUDE.md:53) checks out tag `v0.2.0`, which the upstream owner can move. The tag is also unsigned. Because this skill directs autonomous merges, pin commit `33affa20e5456342ed3a1904bf862616dbf5c42e` and verify `HEAD` after cloning. Current tagged contents contain no executable files or submodules, so the present snapshot shows no direct code-execution concern.

- **Low — bypass description is inaccurate.** [CLAUDE.md:62](/workspaces/sleep-optimizer/CLAUDE.md:62) says the admin bypass is “for the human only,” while the active ruleset grants an always-on bypass to repository role ID 5—an admin-role class, not a named identity. Existing [CLAUDE.md:42](/workspaces/sleep-optimizer/CLAUDE.md:42) still forbids agents from using admin merge, limiting practical impact.

No documented enforcement weakening otherwise. The active ruleset requires PRs, resolved threads, strict/up-to-date status checks, and all six named checks including `review-gate`; approving-review count was already zero. Retiring the CLAUDE.md direct-push exception strengthens the written policy.


--- Post-review delta ---
Fix round applied after review: SHA-pin + verify-existing-install line, faithful escalation paraphrase (bug-fix carve-out + full data item), bypass wording (policy vs enforcement). Attested hash covers the fixed text; delta verified same-section.

findings.summary: Skill pinned by mutable, unsigned tag while directing autonomous merges
findings.reason: Pin is now the commit SHA 33affa2 (independently verified against the tag object and local install); version bumps only via PR editing the pin.

findings.summary: Bypass description inaccurate (role class, not a named identity)
findings.reason: Reworded; converged with the Claude security LOW.
